category_title: Children who speak unintelligibly due to severe phonological impairments
student_description: This student is highly verbal but unintelligible due to severe phonological impairments, yet they suffer from a confident misconception that their speech is perfectly clear. Because they are unaware of their communication breakdowns, they uniquely require explicit notification of being misunderstood to trigger their use of compensatory gestures, alongside long-term exposure to and training in sign-supported communication.

criteria.rationale: Because the student is completely unaware of their unintelligibility, they will only deploy compensatory gestures if a conversational partner explicitly flags that the message was not understood.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly communicates that they did not understand the student's utterance (e.g., asking for clarification, stating 'I don't understand', or prompting the student to repeat).
